title: Documentation: PublishPort does not mention the possibility to define a protocol
body:
### Issue Description

The official documentation (https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html) does not mention, that `PublishPort` can also include the protocol used (`tcp`/`udp`).

[This issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18641#issue-1718237886) mentions `PublishPort=127.0.0.1:51413:51413/udp` as a perfectly valid syntax. I tested it, and it works. But I could not find a source, that would verify this.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Go to https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html and search for `PublishPort`.

### Describe the results you received

No mention of the option to specify a protocol (`tcp`/`udp`).

### Describe the results you expected

An explicit mention, that the protocol (`tcp`/`udp`) can be defined or at least an example, which shows it.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman info                                                                                                                                                        [8:20:01]
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.33.7
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpu
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon_2.1.10+ds1-1build2_amd64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.10, commit: unknown'
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Good catch! It seems it got lost somewhere. Are you interested in creating a PR?

As I would not know if there are more options than `/udp` and `/tcp`, I'd rather have someone else give it a shot.
But if time permits and no one is faster, I might just try and see what comes of it. ⏳
Per - https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d3a81e3e176366f973ee3c719ea6ea31ea7253ad/pkg/specgen/generate/ports.go#L20-L24 - the only options are `tcp`, `udp`, and `sctp`.

Hi, just quickly created a line and copy pasted it in three places.
Will this work?
Open to input
